content:
General
* The clash currently lacks any key programming elements such as lists, sets, dictionaries, string manipulation, loops, conditions…

Please add one or more programming elements so that the clash is more of a programming problem than a maths problem.

You may consider adopting one of the following example tasks (choose only one, not both), or come up with a task of your own:

  • Output the square root if the input is an even integer, and the cube root if the input is an odd integer.

  • Output the cube root if the input is a perfect cube; otherwise, output -1.

* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and format your statement properly as described in the points below.

Goal / Input / Output / Constraints
* Please format "n" as a [[variable]].

Input
* Please format "line 1" in <<bold>>.

Output
* Please format "1 line" in <<bold>>.

* Please change "cuberoot" to "cube root".

Stub generator
* Please change "num" to "n" for consistency with other sections.

* For Reverse mode, please avoid explicitly mentioning "cube root", as this reveals the task instead of allowing the player to infer it.

Thanks.

Thank you for updating the contribution. Please further follow up as follows:

Goal
* Please format all instances of "n" as [[variables]].

Input
* Please change "a number" to "an integer".

Output
* Please format all instances of "n" as [[variables]].

* Please change "cuberoot" to "cube root".

Constraints
* Please format all instances of "n" as [[variables]].

* Please delete the second constraint.

Cases
* 125 (Test 2): Please update the case as the input is 64, not 125.

* 64 (Validator 2): Please revise [[n]] to even and a perfect square instead of 125.

* largerer (Test 4): Please revise [[n]] to even and a perfect square instead of 753571.

* Please add a new pair of cases where [[n]] is even but not a perfect square.

Thanks again.
